exercises.title: Adding Meaningful Comments
exercises.description: The code below works but has no comments. Add a comment above the constants section and above the output section. The code itself should remain unchanged and produce the same output.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Add a comment here explaining what the constants represent
TAX_RATE = 0.08
DAYS_PER_YEAR = 365

price = 50.00
tax_amount = price * TAX_RATE

# TODO: Add a comment here explaining what the output section does
print(f"Price: ${price:.2f}")
print(f"Tax: ${tax_amount:.2f}")
print(f"Total: ${price + tax_amount:.2f}")
hints: Comments start with # and are ignored by Python at runtime.
Good comments explain WHY or WHAT, not just repeat the code.

exercises.title: snake_case Naming
exercises.description: Rewrite the code below using correct Python snake_case naming conventions. The variable names are currently unclear — fix them to be descriptive. The output must remain identical.
exercises.starterCode:
# Fix the variable names to use snake_case and be descriptive
# Current (bad) names: n, s, P
# Change to: student_name, total_score, passing_score

n = "Bob"
s = 87
P = 60

print(f"Student: {n}")
print(f"Score: {s}")
print(f"Passing score: {P}")
hints: Rename n to student_name, s to total_score, P to passing_score.
Update both the variable assignment AND every place it is used.

exercises.title: Constants with ALL_CAPS
exercises.description: Replace the 'magic numbers' in the code with named constants following ALL_CAPS convention. Define the constants first, then use them in the calculation.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Define a constant SECONDS_PER_MINUTE = 60
# TODO: Define a constant MINUTES_PER_HOUR = 60
# TODO: Define a constant HOURS_PER_DAY = 24

# Use the constants to calculate seconds in a day
# seconds_per_day = SECONDS_PER_MINUTE * MINUTES_PER_HOUR * HOURS_PER_DAY
seconds_per_day = 60 * 60 * 24  # Replace with constants

print(f"Seconds in a day: {seconds_per_day}")
hints: Then use: seconds_per_day = SECONDS_PER_MINUTE * MINUTES_PER_HOUR * HOURS_PER_DAY

warmup.title: Quick Recall: PEP 8 Basics
warmup.description: Which version of this code follows PEP 8 style? Both produce the same output — one is correct style.
warmup.starterCode:
# PEP 8 version (spaces around =, descriptive names)
student_age = 20
print(f"The student is {student_age} years old.")
